symptom: Water pooling under or near the indoor air handler. System may still be cooling but running odd. Customer reports a wet ceiling stain (attic install) or water in a closet (horizontal install). Possible musty smell. Float safety switch may have killed the call for cooling entirely.
diagnosis: Clogged primary condensate drain line. Algae or dust buildup in the 3/4in PVC blocks gravity flow. The drain pan overflows through the safety switch port or the side seam of the pan. Float switch, if installed, opens the low-voltage circuit and stops cooling.
procedure: Thermostat OFF. If there's a condensate pump, unplug it (don't let it run dry).
Locate the primary drain line — usually 3/4in PVC exiting the cabinet at or near the pan.
Find the cleanout tee: the capped vertical PVC stub. Usually within 2 feet of the cabinet.
Option A (fastest): wet/dry shop vac on the outdoor termination for 60-90 seconds. Pulls the clog out 90% of the time.
Option B (cleanout access): pour 1 cup distilled white vinegar into the tee. Let sit 10 minutes. Flush with water.
Confirm flow at the outdoor termination. Water should run freely.
Empty standing water from the drain pan with a sponge or shop vac.
Reset the float safety switch: the small cylinder inline on the drain. It resets automatically once water level drops; verify the stat has power again.
Recommend quarterly vinegar maintenance to the customer, or install a condensate pump with integrated safety switch if the gravity run is long or poorly sloped.
safety_notes: Water plus electricity at the air handler blower motor = shock risk. Confirm power off at the disconnect before reaching into a wet cabinet.
Attic units: floor joists may be rot-compromised by prolonged leaks. Test load before kneeling on the deck.
Drain pan mold/bacteria: wear gloves and an N95 if the pan is heavily fouled. Biological aerosols from a neglected drain can trigger respiratory issues.
If the overflow soaked drywall or insulation below the unit, flag a scope change for remediation — that's outside the HVAC repair and needs documentation.
